criteria.label: Open every coding mock with 2–3 non-trivial clarifying questions before writing code.
criteria.example: 'Are values integers or floats? Sorted or unsorted? Can n be 1?' — questions whose answers materially change the approach.

criteria.label: Narrate intent in 1-sentence units while coding.
criteria.example: 'Building a counter for window contents. Expanding r. Shrinking l while distinct > k.'

criteria.label: Visibly budget each phase of a 60-minute mock and stick to it within ±5 min.
criteria.example: '5 min clarify, 10 min approach, 30 min code, 10 min walk-through + complexity, 5 min debrief.'

criteria.label: Recover from a wrong path without ego — restate the better approach and re-budget the remaining time.
criteria.example: 'I see this won't scale to 10⁵; let me switch to a hash map and reset my coding budget to 20 minutes.'

criteria.label: End every mock with a structured debrief that produces ONE concrete drill for the next 2–3 days.
criteria.example: Debrief output: 'I hand-wave at recurrences. Drill: solve one DP problem per day for 5 days, narrating the recurrence aloud.'

criteria.label: Rotate coding, system-design and behavioural mocks across a 4-week cycle.
criteria.example: Week plan: 2 coding mocks + 1 system-design + 1 behavioural; vary partners across the four weeks.
